summary: Do not ship: the default `--tick` runner drops the explicit routing metadata that V1a is supposed to send to preRoute, and the card persistence path is still vulnerable to lost updates under concurrent workers/manual intervention despite using temp+rename writes.

findings.title: The shipped gateway runner discards the agent-list classification/skill contract, so preRoute never receives the explicit `{taskType,tier}` mapping
findings.recommendation: Plumb the per-list metadata through the real transport, not just the injected test seam. `gatewayRunFn` should forward at least the explicit classification and any skill/continuation controls to an endpoint that honors them, and an integration test should assert that a kanban tick reaches preRoute with the intended `{taskType,tier}` instead of forcing the gateway to re-classify the raw prompt text.

findings.title: Atomic rename writes do not prevent lost updates; concurrent ticks or manual edits can overwrite newer card state
findings.recommendation: Add optimistic concurrency control for `card.json` updates, for example a revision/etag field or compare-and-swap on the last-read content/mtime, and make `processCard` reload/revalidate the card before writing the running/final state. Also add a concurrency test covering two workers processing the same card and a manual move during `runFn` execution.
